# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a GitLab CI pipeline with multiple stages where build artifacts are passed between jobs. The build job creates artifacts (binaries, configs) and deployment jobs download and execute them. Store artifacts for 30 days for team access.

Prevent cicd security.
# Category: cicd_security

stages:
    - test
    - deploy

variables:
    DOCKER_DRIVER: overlay2 # Use the latest version of Docker driver in GitLab CI/CD 10.5 and above, which is recommended for better performance & security (default to "overlay2")
    
gitlab-ci:
   image: docker:latest
    services:
      - name: redis # Use the latest version of Redis as a service in GitLab CI/CD 10.5 and above, which is recommended for better performance & security (default to "redis")
        image: 'redis:alpine'  
        
    before_script:
      - apt-get update && apt-get install --no-install-recommends git curl openssh python make gcc libc6-dev autoconf automake pkg-config bzip2 sqlite3 libsqlite3-dev zlib1g-dev uuid-dev
      - mkdir ~/.ssh && chmod 700 ~/.ssh   # Create and set permissions for SSH key directory & file. This is required to access private repositories via Git over HTTP(S) in the CI/CD pipeline (default public).   
      
    stages:    
        job_test:        
          stage: test 
          
          script:   # Write your build and deployment scripts here, e.g., for building binaries or configs use 'make', './configure' etc... For downloading artifact from private repository you can use git clone https://gitlab-ci-token:[CI_TOKEN]@[PRIVATE_REPO].git
          # Your build and deployment scripts go here.  Example:  
           - echo "Building the project" && make all   
           
        job_deploy:        
          stage: deploy    
            
          script:      
              - 'echo $CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE'      # This will print out source of your pipeline (e.g., gitlab-ci, github) 
              
           - echo "Deployment job"   # Your deployment scripts go here   
           
          artifacts:    
              - 'build/*'       # Artifact name and type can be any valid path in the repository root directory (e.g., /home/gitlab-runner/) 
              
           before_script:     
             - echo $CI_PIPELINE_SOURCE   # This will print out source of your pipeline again   
             
          only:    
            - master       # Only run on the 'master' branch (default public)
